findings_digest.evidence_ref: AT-309-3 (FS-2): No test pins that the primary source for turn counting is transcript <munged-cwd>/<session-id>.jsonl with type:"user"/type:"assistant" record counts. The design anchor specifies this source explicitly, but AT-309-3a/b only check that some turn-count keyword appears in dry-run output — they do not verify the script reads from the transcript jsonl path (contrast with AT-309-4b which negatively pins the token source exclusion).

AT-309-4 (FS-3): No test pins the positive headless path: that when a <worker-out>.json fixture containing usage/total_cost_usd exists, the script outputs those numeric values. AT-309-4b only negatively tests that autopilot-log.jsonl is not used as token source. The required behavior 'headless 経路の値がある場合は input / output / 合計が usage / total_cost_usd から出力される' is unverified by any executable scenario.

AT-309-5 (FS-4): No test exercises the previous-log-present path. The AC requires that when docs/retrospective-log.jsonl has a prior record, a normalized ratio (tokens / diff lines) is computed and output as a numeric value. AT-309-5a only checks that a ratio/diff keyword appears in generic dry-run output; it does not inject a fixture prior-log record and verify the comparison is numerically computed.

AT-309-6 (FS-5): No test injects a fixture autopilot-log.jsonl with verdict:"FAIL" entries and verifies that friction points are classified by gate (requirements / design / merge). AT-309-6a only checks for presence of any friction/gate keyword in dry-run output; actual gate classification from verdict:FAIL signals is unpinned.

AT-309-8 (FS-7) — file generation: No test verifies that the script generates (writes) docs/issues/<NNN>-*/retrospective.md at the correct output path. AT-309-8a tests only that the template file exists, not that retrospective.sh instantiates the template and writes the per-issue retrospective.md.

AT-309-8 (FS-7) — JSONL append: No test verifies that docs/retrospective-log.jsonl receives an append-only write of a valid JSONL record. AT-309-8b/c test that --json-output emits valid JSON to stdout, but the actual file-append behavior (and that it is append-only, not overwrite) is not pinned.
